(Alex presents the clue from Jerusalem.) An arch near the first Station of the Cross is called the Ecce Homo Arch, based on the belief that it was here that this Roman governor said, "Behold, the man"

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Alex presents the clue from Jerusalem.) This is the appropriately sorrowful Latin name for the pathway along which Jesus carried the cross

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Alex presents the clue from Jerusalem.) An Armenian church stands at the site of the 4th Station of the Cross where Jesus met her--a fitting site for the hymn closely associated with the way of the cross, the Stabat Mater, "The Mother Was Standing"

(Sarah presents the clue from Jerusalem.) The last of the Stations of the Cross, covered by a slab installed in 1555 & purposely cracked so it wouldn't be looted, is the tomb where Jesus lay for this many days
